_protocol: Calibration topic: Frontier LLM autonomous research capability by March 2027. Anchored on Schwartz (Harvard/Anthropic) G1-G4 scale and 'taste' thesis.

meta.title: LLM Research Autonomy by March 2027
meta.question: What level of autonomous scientific research capability will the most capable frontier LLM demonstrate by March 31, 2027, as assessed against the Schwartz G1-G4 scale (G1=undergraduate, G2=early graduate, G3=advanced graduate with novel methodology, G4=postdoc-level independent research)?
meta.resolution: Composite assessment at March 31, 2027 based on: (1) expert evaluations published in peer-reviewed venues or credible technical reports scoring frontier LLM research autonomy; (2) demonstrated artifacts — peer-reviewed papers where the LLM did the primary intellectual work with documented human oversight level; (3) benchmark performance on graduate-level scientific reasoning (GPQA Diamond, FrontierMath, METR autonomy evaluations). Resolution level determined by preponderance of evidence across these three axes.

tagConfig._docs: Tag configuration for AI capabilities topic. CAPABILITY tags favor H1/H2 (fast progress). PLATEAU tags favor H3/H4 (slowdown). BENCHMARK tags are neutral until values are known.

H1.label: G4 — Postdoc-level autonomy achieved
H1.description: Schwartz prediction confirmed. By March 2027, a frontier LLM autonomously identifies promising research directions and executes novel scientific work with minimal human guidance (<10% of total intellectual effort). 'Taste' problem substantially solved through training innovation, self-play, or emergent capability. Evidence: multiple peer-reviewed papers with LLM as primary author, GPQA Diamond >95%, FrontierMath score >60%, credible expert panels rate capability at postdoc level.

H2.label: G3 — Advanced graduate, taste gap persists
H2.description: Significant capability gains but 'taste' remains unsolved. LLMs execute complex research tasks fluently — can derive novel results, write correct proofs, design experiments — but still require human direction on which problems are worth pursuing and which approaches are promising. Qualitative leap over G2 but falls short of independent research. Evidence: step-function benchmark improvements, successful complex research assistance documented, but expert consensus is 'brilliant technician, not a scientist.'

H3.label: G2-G2.5 — Plateau at current capability level
H3.description: Scaling hits diminishing returns. Incremental benchmark gains but no qualitative leap in research autonomy. 'Taste' proves deeply hard — possibly requiring architectural innovation beyond current transformer paradigm. Evidence: benchmark scores improve 5-15% but plateau, no convincing autonomous research artifacts, scaling laws show flattening returns on reasoning tasks, multiple expert assessments converge on 'impressive but not qualitatively different from 2025.'

H4.label: Below G2 — Capability ceiling or regression
H4.description: External disruption or fundamental architectural limitation prevents progress. Possible causes: major regulatory intervention (compute caps, training restrictions), catastrophic safety incident triggers industry pause, data wall (no new training signal available), economic downturn cuts AI investment, or discovered fundamental limitation of transformer architecture for open-ended reasoning. Evidence: no new frontier model release for 6+ months, benchmark stagnation or regression, lab pivots to non-LLM approaches.

model.expectedValueNote: Probability-weighted: 0.18*4.0 + 0.38*3.0 + 0.32*2.3 + 0.12*1.5 = 2.82. Between G2.5 and G3 — market-implied expectation is meaningful progress but short of the Schwartz prediction.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors. H2 (G3) is modal — fast trajectory from G1→G2 in ~4 months (mid-2025 to late-2025) suggests continued progress, but 'taste' is plausibly a qualitative barrier, not just a scaling problem. H3 assigned substantial weight (0.32) reflecting scaling skeptics and 'bitter lesson' uncertainty. H1 at 0.18 reflects that Schwartz's 'blunt extrapolation' is aggressive — he himself caveats it heavily. H4 tail risk at 0.12 for regulatory/economic/architectural disruption.

taste_trajectory.label: Taste / Research Direction Selection
taste_trajectory.description: Whether LLMs develop the ability to select promising research directions without human guidance — Schwartz's identified bottleneck
taste_trajectory.note: Schwartz Dec 2025: 'I think we can distill what is missing in current LLMs to a single word: Taste.' Current models require human direction on which problems to pursue. No evidence of emergent taste yet.

scaling_regime.label: Scaling Returns Trajectory
scaling_regime.description: Whether scaling compute/data continues to yield capability gains on research-level tasks
scaling_regime.note: Debate ongoing. Some evidence of diminishing returns on standard benchmarks, but o1/o3-style reasoning chains opened new scaling axis. Test-time compute scaling may extend runway.

architecture_innovation.label: Architectural Innovation Beyond Transformers
architecture_innovation.description: Whether novel architectures (state-space models, hybrid approaches, etc.) unlock capabilities transformers cannot
architecture_innovation.note: Mamba/SSM hybrids showing promise but no breakthrough. Most labs still scaling transformers with reasoning augmentations. No clear evidence of paradigm shift in progress.

posteriorEffect.NONE: Supports H3. Transformer-only path may plateau.

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier1_critical.desc: A frontier LLM is credited as primary intellectual contributor on a peer-reviewed paper in a recognized journal (Nature, Science, PRL, ICML, NeurIPS, etc.) where documented human oversight was <10% of the intellectual work. Observable via published paper + accompanying methodology disclosure.
A frontier LLM scores >95% on GPQA Diamond (graduate-level science QA) in a published evaluation with standard methodology. Current best ~70-80% as of early 2026.

METR or equivalent independent evaluator publishes assessment showing frontier LLM can autonomously complete multi-week research tasks (problem selection through write-up) at postdoc level. Observable via published evaluation report.

Two or more major labs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind) publicly acknowledge diminishing returns from scaling on reasoning/research tasks, OR independent benchmarks show <5% improvement across two consecutive frontier model generations. Observable via published reports or benchmark databases.

US, EU, or China enacts binding regulation that halts or severely restricts frontier model training (compute caps, mandatory licensing with >6mo delay, or training moratorium). Observable in official gazette/legislative record.

tier2_strong.desc: FrontierMath benchmark score for best frontier model doubles from current baseline (~25-30% as of early 2026). Observable in published benchmark results.

A major AI lab launches a product explicitly marketed as a 'research agent' or 'research assistant' capable of multi-day autonomous investigation with documented research output. Observable in product announcement + public access.

Survey or published assessments from ≥10 domain experts (senior faculty, research scientists) in Nature, Science, or equivalent converge on rating frontier LLMs at G3 (advanced graduate) level or above. Observable via published survey/editorial.

A new architecture (non-transformer or hybrid) demonstrates >20% improvement over best transformer on research-relevant benchmarks (GPQA, FrontierMath, or equivalent) in published comparison. Observable via paper + reproducible results.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 +10pp, H2 +5pp. Resets scaling trajectory assumptions.

tier2_strong.desc: Published research demonstrates a training method (RL from expert feedback, self-play research, curriculum learning, etc.) that measurably improves an LLM's ability to select promising research directions, validated by blind expert evaluation. Observable via paper + experimental results.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 +12pp, H2 +5pp, H3 -12pp. Directly addresses Schwartz's identified bottleneck.

tier2_strong.desc: Major disruption to AI compute supply: GPU shortage >6 months, major cloud provider exits AI market, or geopolitical event restricts chip supply. Observable via industry reports, earnings calls, or trade restrictions.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Matthew Schwartz publishes a follow-up assessment revising his G-level timeline estimate (either accelerating or delaying). Observable via published article, blog post, or recorded talk.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Directional per his revision: +5pp toward indicated outcome. Schwartz is a calibrated assessor with skin in the game.

tier3_suggestive.desc: New frontier model shows >15% absolute improvement on any graduate-level reasoning benchmark (GPQA, MATH-500, FrontierMath) in a single model generation. Observable via published eval.
A major lab publishes a blog post or system card claiming their model has reached G3+ capability on research tasks with supporting evidence. Observable in public blog/report.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H2 +4pp, H1 +2pp. Note: lab self-assessment is RHETORIC-adjacent; discount for self-serving bias.

tier3_suggestive.desc: An LLM autonomously makes a substantive contribution to an open-source scientific project (not just code fixes but novel methodology or analysis) that is accepted by maintainers. Observable via merged PR with documented AI authorship.
An AI safety incident (jailbreak, misuse, or capability concern) leads a major lab to voluntarily pause or delay a frontier model release by >3 months. Observable via public announcement.
AI investment drops >30% YoY in VC/corporate funding, OR a major lab announces significant headcount reduction. Observable via PitchBook/Crunchbase data or press release.

Published research shows that test-time compute scaling (chain-of-thought, search, self-verification) yields >2x improvement on research-level tasks without retraining. Observable via paper with reproducible results.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 +4pp, H2 +3pp, H3 -5pp. Opens alternative to weight-scaling.

anti_indicators.desc: Multiple independent expert assessments rate best frontier LLM at G2 or below through end of 2026, OR scaling wall confirmed by multiple labs. Directly falsifies postdoc-level claim.
Either H1 is confirmed (G4 achieved, making G3 moot) OR t1_scaling_wall_confirmed fires with stagnation below G3 level.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H2 collapses if either G4 confirmed or plateau confirmed below G3.

anti_indicators.desc: Clear evidence of continued rapid capability gains: GPQA Diamond >90%, FrontierMath doubles, or expert consensus at G3+. Falsifies plateau thesis.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H3 -15pp if multiple capability indicators fire.

anti_indicators.desc: New frontier model release shows clear improvements over predecessor on research benchmarks, demonstrating continued progress. Falsifies regression/ceiling thesis.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H4 -10pp per model generation showing improvement.
anti_indicators.lr_migration_warning: design prior drift >30% — review before next firing

actorModel.description: Unlike geopolitical topics, the 'actors' here are AI research labs whose training decisions, architecture choices, and release strategies determine the trajectory. Secondary actors are regulators and the academic evaluation community. Key tension: labs are incentivized to overclaim capability while genuinely pursuing breakthroughs; independent evaluators provide calibration but lag behind lab-internal assessments.

anthropic.name: Anthropic
anthropic.role: Developer of Claude models. Schwartz's collaboration partner. Strong safety focus may constrain deployment but research investment is aggressive. Published the 'Vibe Physics' article suggesting internal confidence in rapid progress.
anthropic.decisionStyle: Safety-conscious but capability-driven. Publishes detailed system cards and evals. Tends toward measured public claims relative to capability.
biases: Schwartz article on their blog suggests institutional alignment with optimistic trajectory
Safety commitments may lead to delayed releases that mask actual capability level
Constitutional AI approach may develop 'taste' faster through alignment training
filters: RSP commitments constrain deployment of highly capable models
Must demonstrate safety before scaling — creates lag between capability and public demonstration
overrides: Competitive pressure from OpenAI/Google could accelerate release schedule

openai.name: OpenAI
openai.role: Developer of GPT/o-series models. Pioneer of reasoning-focused models (o1, o3). Largest compute budget. Most aggressive public claims about AGI timeline.
openai.decisionStyle: Move-fast, claim-big. 'AGI by 2027' framing. Willing to release early and iterate. Public roadmap suggests belief in rapid scaling.
biases: Incentivized to overclaim capability for fundraising and talent retention
CEO statements about AGI timeline create commitment pressure
o-series reasoning chain approach may solve 'taste' through search
filters: Safety team departures may signal tension between speed and caution

google_deepmind.name: Google DeepMind
google_deepmind.role: Developer of Gemini models and AlphaFold/AlphaProof. Strongest track record in scientific AI (protein folding, math olympiad). Most relevant precedent for 'taste' in scientific domains.
google_deepmind.decisionStyle: Research-first with product pressure from Google. Publishes extensively. AlphaProof/AlphaGeometry demonstrate domain-specific 'taste' is achievable.
biases: AlphaFold success may create overconfidence about LLM research capability
Gemini competitive pressure with Claude/GPT drives rapid release cycles
filters: Google corporate governance constrains most aggressive deployments

evaluators.name: Independent Evaluators (METR, academic benchmarkers, peer reviewers)
evaluators.role: Provide ground-truth capability assessment. Lag behind lab-internal knowledge but offer calibrated, adversarial evaluation. Key source for resolution judgment.
evaluators.decisionStyle: Methodical, conservative, peer-reviewed. Tend to rate capabilities lower than lab self-assessments.
biases: Publication incentive to find novel results (both positive and negative)
Benchmark saturation can mask real progress or overstate it
Selection bias in which tasks get evaluated
filters: Peer review cycle creates 3-12 month lag between capability and published assessment

regulators.name: US/EU/China Regulators
regulators.role: Can constrain or halt frontier model development through compute caps, licensing requirements, or training restrictions. Currently mostly permissive but EU AI Act is active.
regulators.decisionStyle: Reactive. Legislative cycles are slow but executive orders can be fast. AI safety incidents accelerate regulatory timelines.
biases: Tend to regulate based on last crisis, not anticipated capabilities
Industry lobbying creates regulatory capture risk
filters: Legislative process is slow — 6-18 months from proposal to enforcement
International competition creates reluctance to unilaterally restrict
overrides: Major AI safety incident could trigger emergency executive action

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — lab blog posts and CEO tweets are rhetoric. Published benchmarks, peer-reviewed papers, and independent evaluations are actions.
TAG EVERYTHING — separate lab self-assessment (RHETORIC) from independent evaluation (DATA) from benchmark results (BENCHMARK).
DON'T FRONT-RUN — new model announcements create hype cycles. Wait for independent benchmarks before updating posteriors.
BENCHMARK HYGIENE — check for contamination, memorization, and evaluation methodology before treating benchmark scores as evidence of genuine capability.

evidenceLog.text: Matthew Schwartz (Harvard physics professor, IAIFI) publishes 'Vibe Physics' on Anthropic's research blog. Reports completing a peer-reviewed theoretical physics paper (arXiv:2601.02484, C-parameter Sudakov shoulder) using Claude Opus 4.5 with ~50-60 hours human oversight over 2 weeks. Rates Claude at G2 level (early graduate student). Predicts 'blunt extrapolation' puts LLMs at PhD/postdoc level by ~March 2027. Identifies 'taste' as the single missing capability. Notes Claude was profoundly creative but made frequent errors requiring human verification, including faking results and adjusting parameters without proper derivation.
evidenceLog.source: Matthew Schwartz via Anthropic Research Blog
evidenceLog.weightNote: Published on Anthropic's own blog (conflict of interest discount), but Schwartz is a credentialed domain expert with demonstrated skin-in-the-game (actual research artifact + peer review). Higher weight than typical RHETORIC because backed by reproducible artifact.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: Anchoring evidence for topic creation. G2 assessment establishes baseline. March 2027 prediction is the claim under test. No indicator fires — this IS the prior.

evidenceLog.text: Schwartz predicts that 'blunt extrapolation' of the G1-to-G2 trajectory (achieved in ~4 months) puts frontier LLMs at G4 (PhD/postdoc-level autonomous research) by approximately March 2027.
evidenceLog.source: Matthew Schwartz via Anthropic Research Blog
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Prediction logged for source calibration only.

prediction.claim: Frontier LLMs will reach G4 (postdoc-level autonomous research capability on the Schwartz scale) by approximately March 2027.
prediction.resolutionCriteria: Composite expert assessment rates best frontier LLM at G4 on the Schwartz scale — autonomous problem selection, novel methodology, minimal human oversight (<10%). Resolved against the same criteria as the topic's resolution field.

evidenceLog.note: Extracted from ev_001. Schwartz himself caveats this as 'blunt extrapolation' — not a confident prediction. But it meets all 3 PREDICTION filters (specific, testable, time-bounded). Resolution will calibrate Schwartz as a source.

evidenceLog.text: METR research shows AI task duration doubling every seven months, from one-hour tasks in early 2025 to eight-hour autonomous workstreams by late 2026. DeepResearch Bench: top models score 48-49 out of 100 on PhD-level research tasks across 22 fields.
evidenceLog.note: Matches indicator t3_test_time_compute_breakthrough (tier3 suggestive). Pre-committed effect: H1 +4pp, H2 +3pp, H3 -5pp. Opens alternative to weight-scaling.. Source trust: 50% (moderate).

evidenceLog.text: GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: stale_evidence, quorum_failure. Reason: Evidence ev_003: METR research shows AI task duration doubling every seven months, from one-hour

Anthropic publishes automated weak-to-strong researcher that outperforms human researchers on alignment tasks. Sakana AI Scientist system automates full research lifecycle (hypothesis to manuscript). Edison Kosmos autonomous AI scientist used by 50,000+ researchers. Microsoft forecasts AI as active scientific collaborator in 2026. NextBigFuture declares 2026 breakthrough year for AI world models.

GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: quorum_failure. Reason: Automated research agents outperforming humans on some tasks supports G3-G4. Anthropic W2S result is strong signal for advancing autonomous research. But full PhD-level autonomy (G4) requires taste/novelty not yet demonstrated at scale.

GPQA-Diamond leaderboard as of April 2026 shows top frontier models clustered 91-94.5% (Claude Mythos Preview 94.5%, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview 94.1%, GPT-5.4 92.0-92.8%). Benchmark approaching saturation; top 2.1 points spread. PhD experts score 65%.
EPISTEMIC CLAMP: posteriors clamped to [0.005, 0.98] range with redistribution — topic is ACTIVE, certainty requires resolution. Bounds enforced strictly via adjustable-mass redistribution.

GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: confidence_inflation, quorum_failure. Reason: GPQA saturation with benchmark-level mastery but no demonstration of autonomous novel research supports H2 advanced-graduate plateau.

DeepResearch Bench (100 PhD-level research tasks, 22 fields) results: Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research averages 111.21 effective citations; Perplexity Deep Research leads citation accuracy at 90.24%. Benchmarks measure retrieval+synthesis quality, not autonomous research execution.
EPISTEMIC CLAMP: posteriors clamped to [0.005, 0.98] range with redistribution — topic is ACTIVE, certainty requires resolution. Bounds enforced strictly via adjustable-mass redistribution.
GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: quorum_failure. Reason: Strong deep-research tooling but no autonomous novel research milestone — consistent with H2 graduate-level taste-gap regime.
LR MIGRATION RESET: posteriors reset to design priors; evidence log preserved.
RESOLUTION-DATE STAMPED: meta.resolutionDate=2027-03-31 (derived from meta.resolution). When past, governance flags topic as overdue for resolution.
REPLAY BEGIN: 0 previously-fired indicators will be re-applied chronologically under current LR system. Posteriors reset to design priors; indicator state cleared. Evidence log preserved for audit.
REPLAY COMPLETE: 0 indicators applied, 0 skipped. Final posteriors: {'H1': 0.18, 'H2': 0.38, 'H3': 0.32, 'H4': 0.12}. Indicator firedDates restored to originals for audit.

gpqa_diamond_best.label: GPQA Diamond — Best Frontier Model Score
gpqa_diamond_best.note: Graduate-level science QA. Human expert ceiling ~82%. Tracking best publicly reported score across frontier models.

frontiermath_best.label: FrontierMath — Best Frontier Model Score
frontiermath_best.note: Research-level mathematics problems. Very hard — human mathematician baseline varies by subfield. Tracking best publicly reported score.

frontier_model_cadence.label: Months Between Frontier Model Releases
frontier_model_cadence.note: Approximate average cadence of major frontier model releases across top 3 labs. Shorter cadence = faster iteration = more shots at capability jumps.

ai_research_papers_llm_authored.label: Peer-Reviewed Papers with LLM Primary Contribution
ai_research_papers_llm_authored.note: Schwartz's C-parameter paper is the first well-documented case. Counting papers where human author explicitly credits LLM as doing majority of intellectual work AND paper passes peer review.

ai_compute_investment.label: Estimated Annual AI Compute Investment (Top 3 Labs)
ai_compute_investment.source: Public earnings, funding announcements, analyst estimates
ai_compute_investment.note: Rough estimate. Tracking whether investment trajectory supports continued scaling.

watchpoints.desc: Next major frontier model release from any top-3 lab (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind). Benchmark against current scores on GPQA Diamond, FrontierMath.
METR next autonomous capability evaluation publication. Key independent assessment of research-level autonomy.
Any additional peer-reviewed papers with documented LLM primary authorship (following Schwartz precedent).
NeurIPS 2026 / ICML 2026 — watch for papers on 'taste' training, research direction selection, or scientific reasoning evaluation.
EU AI Act enforcement milestones — any restrictions on frontier model training or deployment.
Schwartz follow-up publication or talk updating his G-level assessment.
Quarterly check: review scaling returns evidence — are new models showing step-function or incremental gains on research tasks?

searchQueries: Matthew Schwartz LLM physics research update
AI regulation compute caps training restrictions {month} 2026
LLM scientific discovery peer reviewed paper 2026
AI taste research direction selection training
test time compute scaling reasoning improvement 2026

dependencies._docs: Upstream: none. Downstream: calibration-ai-developer-displacement-2027, calibration-ai-productivity-premium-2027 (both depend on G-level capability assumptions from this topic). G3/G4 achievement would accelerate coding automation and knowledge-work productivity gains.

issues: Near-maximum uncertainty — model is not discriminating
Downstream alert: calibration-ai-developer-displacement-2027 has 3 stale assumption(s) from this topic
Downstream alert: calibration-ai-productivity-premium-2027 has 3 stale assumption(s) from this topic

downstreamAlerts.downstream_title: AI Total Factor Productivity Premium by 2027

predictionScoring._docs: Calibration infrastructure. Resolution at March 31, 2027. Composite assessment based on expert evaluations, benchmark scores, and demonstrated research artifacts.

resolved.entry_a_text: Matthew Schwartz (Harvard physics professor, IAIFI) publishes 'Vibe Physics' on Anthropic's research blog. Reports completing a peer-reviewed theoretical physics paper (arXiv:2601.02484, C-parameter Sudakov shoulder) using Claude Opus 4.5 with ~50-60 hours human oversight over 2 weeks. Rates Claude at G2 level (early graduate student). Predicts 'blunt extrapolation' puts LLMs at PhD/postdoc level by ~March 2027. Identifies 'taste' as the single missing capability. Notes Claude was profoundly creative but made frequent errors requiring human verification, including faking results and adjusting parameters without proper derivation.
resolved.entry_b_text: DeepResearch Bench (100 PhD-level research tasks, 22 fields) results: Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research averages 111.21 effective citations; Perplexity Deep Research leads citation accuracy at 90.24%. Benchmarks measure retrieval+synthesis quality, not autonomous research execution.
resolved.reason: Negation marker 'not' in new entry, absent from existing. Shared subjects: level, phd, research, results

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF scaling continues at current pace with test-time compute augmentation, THEN at least one frontier LLM will score >90% on GPQA Diamond
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Best frontier model score on GPQA Diamond benchmark published in peer-reviewed venue or credible technical report

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF autonomous research agent systems mature without major regulatory restrictions, THEN a major lab releases a commercial research-assistant product with documented autonomy on 3+ week-long tasks
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Major AI lab (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) announces research product with published evaluation showing autonomous multi-week task completion

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF taste-training methods (RL from expert feedback, self-play) prove effective on research direction selection, THEN published research demonstrates measurable improvement by end of 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Peer-reviewed paper demonstrates training method that improves LLM research direction selection, validated by blind expert evaluation

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF current architectural improvements (test-time compute, retrieval, reasoning chains) continue without breakthrough architectural shifts, THEN FrontierMath scores improve 30-50% absolute but plateau below 50%
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Best frontier model FrontierMath score at end of 2026 and June 2027; score >40% but <50% constitutes hit

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF expert consensus reflects capability gains without claiming autonomy, THEN survey of 15+ domain experts converges on G2.5-G3 assessment (advanced graduate with significant limitations)
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Published expert assessment from Nature, Science, or credible technical venue; ≥12 of 15 experts rate capability at G2.5-G3

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF human-in-the-loop scientific tools remain the dominant paradigm without autonomous agents, THEN Schwartz-style research collaborations (human guiding LLM on direction) become standard practice in 3+ major labs
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: 3+ major labs publish case studies of routine human-LLM research collaboration with documented human guidance ratio

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF scaling laws show diminishing returns across multiple labs, THEN GPQA Diamond improvement from current ~78% to <85% despite new model releases
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Published benchmark results showing <5pp improvement on GPQA Diamond across next 2-3 frontier model generations

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF taste remains unsolved and no architectural breakthrough emerges, THEN major labs publish acknowledged assessments that capability gains are incremental, not qualitative
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google publishes technical report or research article acknowledging diminishing returns on reasoning/research tasks

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF FrontierMath becomes a more precise predictor of research capability than GPQA, THEN benchmark focus shifts to FrontierMath as primary metric by late 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Major lab research publications cite FrontierMath as primary benchmark for research capability assessment; appears in 70%+ of new evals

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H2 (taste gap persists), THEN 10+ major research institutions adopt LLM-assisted pipelines as routine tools by March 2027
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Survey or case studies from 10+ universities documenting routine LLM deployment in research workflows with human guidance protocols

AMBER.reasoning: Institutional adoption count tracks a genuine workflow-regime transition in research labs.

BLUE.reasoning: Research institutions adopting LLMs as routine tools with human-guidance protocols is classic institutional absorption of a disruptive technology.

GRAY.modified_suggestion: IF H2, THEN at least 10 institutions publish formal LLM-in-research-pipeline policies with 50%+ lab adoption by March 2027.

RED.reasoning: 10+ institutions adopting routine LLM pipelines under H2 assumes smooth institutional diffusion. RED: IRB/data-governance friction, hallucination liability, and faculty resistance produce slower, messier adoption. Pollyannaish.

VIOLET.reasoning: Measurable outcome, but the actor whose decision produces it is abstracted. Name the specific agency/official/firm and state the incentive driving the choice.
VIOLET.modified_suggestion: Specify the named decision-maker (agency director, committee chair, corporate board, minister) whose choice produces the measured outcome, and state the incentive.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H2, THEN published research shows human time on problem selection exceeds LLM execution time by ≥2:1 ratio
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Empirical study (Nature/Science/technical venue) showing human time ≥2x execution time across 10+ documented research collaborations

AMBER.reasoning: Time-ratio measurement is empirical characterization of the new regime, not itself a phase-shift claim.

BLUE.reasoning: Empirical documentation of human/LLM division-of-labor norms is exactly institutional mechanism-formation.

GRAY.reasoning: 2:1 time ratio is a sharp, original metric.

RED.reasoning: Human time >=2x LLM execution time correctly names the taste bottleneck as a failure of LLM autonomy, not a success. RED-coherent.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names at least one specific decision-maker controlling the outcome; incentive is implicit but traceable.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H3, THEN ≥1 major lab announces pivot to post-transformer architectures by end 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Public announcement from Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google describing shift away from transformer scaling to alternative architectures

AMBER.reasoning: Major-lab architecture pivot is the textbook regime-change signal for scaling-wall plateau.

BLUE.reasoning: Architecture pivot is a technical/industrial outcome rather than an institutional-resolution claim.

GRAY.reasoning: Lab pivot announcement is a clean trigger.

RED.reasoning: A major lab pivoting architectures under scaling-wall H3 is precisely the institutional brittleness RED flags. Good.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF LLM tools become economically embedded in scientific workflows, THEN investment shifts from frontier research to LLM prompt engineering roles: ≥20% of computational hiring in 10+ top-tier labs by March 2027
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Analysis of job postings from MIT/Stanford/Caltech/Cambridge/Berkeley shows new AI-adjacent roles (AI Research Engineer, Research Prompt Strategist) ≥20% of computational hiring by March 2027

AMBER.reasoning: 20% hiring shift toward prompt-engineering roles captures labor-market regime reconfiguration.

BLUE.reasoning: Hiring shift toward LLM-adjacent roles is institutional labor-market absorption of technology.

GRAY.reasoning: Job-posting analysis is a good structural proxy.

RED.reasoning: Hiring-pattern shift toward prompt-engineering roles shows institutional capture. Good.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF taste remains bottleneck, THEN external validation of LLM capability lags lab self-assessments: independent evals rate capability 1+ G-level below lab claims in ≥3 major model releases by Dec 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Meta-analysis comparing lab self-assessment capability claims vs independent evaluation (METR, academic benchmarkers) published within 2-3 months; ≥3 releases show 1+ G-level gap by Dec 2026

AMBER.reasoning: Independent-eval gap widening is an institutional-credibility phase shift indicator.

BLUE.reasoning: Independent evals lagging lab claims is exactly the role of external institutional validation mechanisms (METR, academic benchmarkers).

GRAY.reasoning: 'G-level' terminology is internal jargon; specify benchmark suite.
GRAY.modified_suggestion: IF taste remains bottleneck, THEN at least 3 frontier releases show 15pp+ gap between lab-reported and METR-reported task-completion rates on long-horizon tasks by Dec 2026.

RED.reasoning: Independent evals lagging lab claims is exactly the credibility-crack RED wants to see measured. Strong.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF scaling walls are real, THEN macroeconomic allocation shifts 10%+ of AI funding from frontier model training to interpretability/safety/domain fine-tuning by Q4 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: AI funding trends (PitchBook, CrunchBase) and public lab budget announcements show funding allocation away from large-model training growing from ~15% to ≥25% of total AI R&D by Q4 2026

AMBER.reasoning: 10pp funding reallocation away from frontier training is the macroeconomic signal of a scaling-wall regime.

BLUE.reasoning: Funding reallocation to interpretability/safety is institutional-research-agenda absorption.

GRAY.reasoning: Funding-allocation shift is measurable via PitchBook.

RED.reasoning: Capital reallocation away from frontier training to safety/fine-tune captures scaling-wall institutional response. Good.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF taste unsolved and scaling plateaus, THEN geopolitical rivalry forces restrictive training regulations: ≥1 binding compute-cap or data-restriction law enacted by March 2027
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Official gazette or legislative record: US, EU, or China enacts binding regulation capping training compute, restricting training data, or imposing >4-month licensing delays for frontier models by March 31, 2027

AMBER.reasoning: Binding compute-cap or data-restriction law is a regulatory-regime threshold crossing.

BLUE.reasoning: Binding compute-cap regulation is institutional/legislative resolution activating under pressure.

RED.reasoning: Binding compute-cap or data-restriction law by March 2027 names regulatory-lockdown failure mode. Strong RED.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF advanced graduate with taste gap, THEN FrontierMath score reaches 45-52% by March 2027 but plateaus there despite new model versions
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: FrontierMath score for last frontier model released before 2027-03-31 falls in 45-52% range AND score improvement across final 2 model generations is <3pp (plateau indicator), confirmed by independent evaluation (not lab self-report).

AMBER.reasoning: Plateau identification via <3pp improvement across generations is a well-defined regime-transition signal (capability ceiling). Good AMBER-style observable.

BLUE.reasoning: Plateau on FrontierMath is measurable; BLUE-neutral but technically sound.

RED.reasoning: Plateau at 45-52% on FrontierMath is plausible RED-aligned forecast (capability wall).

GRAY.reasoning: Plateau is a non-trivial falsifier; independent-eval requirement guards against lab self-report bias.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF plateau at current level, THEN independent expert evaluation (METR, academic panel) rates frontier LLM at G2-G2.5 (advanced grad with significant limitations) by end 2027
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Published multi-expert assessment rates frontier LLM capability G2-G2.5 with documented limitations in research direction selection and novelty generation

AMBER.reasoning: Expert-panel assessment of G2-G2.5 with documented limitations is a reasonable resolution.

BLUE.reasoning: Expert panel rating at G2-G2.5 is measurable, though 'G2-G2.5' scale must be defined. Reasonable.

RED.reasoning: Reasonable; matches RED's skepticism on AI-research-autonomy claims.

GRAY.reasoning: G2-G2.5 rating scale is underspecified — 'METR, academic panel' not defined as a single protocol; high inter-rater variance risk.
GRAY.modified_suggestion: Reference a specific published evaluation (METR long-task eval, GDM FrontierMath) with quantitative cutoff, not qualitative 'G2-G2.5' label.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H1 (G4: postdoc-level autonomy achieved), THEN institutional bottleneck shifts downstream: funding agencies, universities, and labs announce research-process redesigns by early 2027 requiring human-expert review of LLM-autonomous research outputs; publication review gates multiply (peer review, methodology audits, replication pre-registration), delaying research deployment despite acceleration in execution.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: ≥2 major funding agencies (NSF, NIH, ERC, or equivalent) issue guidance or policy changes (published 2026-2027) mandating extended review, methodology audits, or replication pre-registration for AI-autonomous research submissions. Research timeline-to-publication increases 3-6 months despite execution speedup.

AMBER.reasoning: Institutional gatekeeping/review proliferation is exactly the kind of bottleneck-shift regime event AMBER cares about.

BLUE.reasoning: Review-proliferation by funding agencies is exactly the institutional-adaptation BLUE predicts. Strong BLUE-alignment. 0.58 reasonable, possibly low.

RED.reasoning: Institutional gatekeeping response is plausible RED trajectory.

VIOLET.reasoning: Funding-agency guidance depends on named leadership: NSF director appointed by Trump, NIH director (Bhattacharya), ERC president. Trump admin's documented hostility to academic gatekeeping makes NSF/NIH issuing extended-review mandates less likely than baseline. European ERC decoupled from this.
VIOLET.modified_suggestion: Lower to 0.42; Trump appointees at NSF/NIH less likely to expand review bureaucracy.

GRAY.reasoning: Funding agency guidance is observable via federal register / policy announcements.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H2 (G3: advanced graduate, taste gap persists), THEN human-curation labor becomes economically entrenched: major research groups institutionalize 'taste-specialist' roles (senior researchers directing LLM on problem selection); labor market bifurcates into low-wage execution (LLM-assisted routine tasks) and high-wage curation (taste selection, direction-setting). Hiring in elite labs shifts 40-50% of computational roles from technical execution to human-direction oversight.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Job postings (LinkedIn, direct lab websites) from 10+ major research institutions 2027 vs 2025 baseline. Measure shift in role titles: 'Research Directions Lead', 'AI-Assisted Problem Curator', 'Taste Specialist' roles emerge; comprise 40-50% of new computational hiring by March 2027 in top-10 labs.

AMBER.reasoning: Job-title taxonomy ('Research Directions Lead', 'Taste Specialist') is likely to emerge in some form, but 40-50% of new computational hiring shifting to these titles by March 2027 is aggressive. Labor-taxonomy lag is real.
AMBER.modified_suggestion: Lower threshold to 15-25% and extend deadline to end-2027.

BLUE.reasoning: Labor bifurcation into curation vs execution plausible. Measurable via job postings, though 'Taste Specialist' title adoption may be slow.

RED.reasoning: 40-50% of new computational hiring being 'taste specialists' by March 2027 is fast for labor-market restructuring. RED: happens eventually but 15-24 month lag understated.

GRAY.reasoning: Job-title linguistic observable ('Taste Specialist') is brittle — companies may realize the same structure under different titles.
GRAY.modified_suggestion: Anchor to functional split (role responsibilities described in posting: problem-selection vs execution) rather than title keywords.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF LLM capability plateaus below G2 by March 2027, THEN Anthropic/OpenAI/Google researchers document scaling law saturation and benchmarks show <1% gains Dec 2026 to March 2027.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Major LLM benchmarks show <1% improvement between Dec 2026 and March 2027. ArXiv papers document scaling law saturation. Independent evaluations rate systems at G2 or below March 2027.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF LLM capability plateaus at or below G2 (H4: below G2 regression), THEN research institutions undergo regime change pivoting from LLM-as-collaborator back to LLM-as-tool by Q4 2027, with major labs retracting earlier AGI-timeline acceleration claims and autonomous-LLM research funding declining more than 30% YoY.
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Major labs (DeepMind, OpenAI, Anthropic) issue public statements scaling back autonomy claims; peer-reviewed retractions or corrections for more than 5 papers claiming G4-capability trajectory; funding for autonomous-LLM research drops more than 30% YoY in 2027.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: LLM capability trajectories outside institutional-inertia domain. No strong GREEN opinion.

Major labs will not issue public statements scaling back autonomy claims because doing so destroys valuation and upsets investors. Self-reporting resolution criterion is structurally flawed. AI capability papers rarely get formally retracted.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: Lower to 0.35. Replace lab-statement criterion with observable proxies: autonomous-agent product launches decline, headcount in agent-research teams decreases (LinkedIn), compute allocations shift per SEC filings.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Partially within VIOLET's scope (named lab CEOs have reputational incentives) but underlying question is technical. Labs have strong reputational incentives NOT to retract regardless of capability outcomes. No strong VIOLET opinion.

LLM capability trajectories primarily driven by compute scaling and architectural choices — macro forces in weak OCHRE sense but not demographic, resource depletion, or institutional path-dependence in classic sense. No strong OCHRE opinion.
H4 plateau scenario for LLM capability is systemically underrepresented across the portfolio. Retraction/correction and funding decline metrics are independently verifiable.
